---
applyTo: "**/*.ts,**/*.tsx"
---

# React i18n Rules

## 1. Message Location

- All UI text (messages, labels, tooltips, etc.) MUST be defined in a `message.tsx` file located in the same directory or a parent directory of the component.

## 2. defaultMessage Restrictions

- The `defaultMessage` field MUST NOT contain any HTML tags.
- Only placeholder tags are allowed in `defaultMessage`. Allowed placeholders (to be confirmed):
  - `<b>...</b>`
  - `<a>...</a>`
  - (Add more as needed)

## 3. Message ID Naming

- `id` MUST follow folder-level naming based on location.
  - View-level example: `view.home.welcome`
  - Component-level example: `components.header.title`
  - Derive the path from the file/folder hierarchy for consistent lookup.

### Example: defaultMessage usage

```typescript
// message.tsx
import { defineMessages } from 'react-intl';

export const messages = defineMessages({
  welcome: {
    id: 'view.home.welcome',
    defaultMessage: 'Welcome, <b>{userName}</b>!',
  },
  link: {
    id: 'components.header.link',
    defaultMessage: 'Click <a>{linkText}</a> to continue.',
  },
});
```
